Notice Of Motion And Motion For Default Judgment Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Aug-20-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was set for Prove- Up for a Request for Default Judgment against Defendant Roman Orlov and Defendant Regina Orlov.

Dept.505: Judge: Michelle Tong; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceedings reported by "For the Record", commencing at 9:39:44 a.m and concluding at 10:00:52 a.m. (D505)

Milla L. Lovovich, Esq. representing Plaintiff Yakov Pesochinsky who was present, placed under oath and testified via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Roman Orlov and Defendant Regina Orlov not appearing.

Complaint filed on May-24-2024.
Proof of service filed as to Defendants on Jun-28-2024.
Default entered as to both defendants on Aug-27-2024 and Mar-03-2025.
Defendant Does 1 through 10 were dismissed on Mar-03-2025.

Counsel proceeds by offer of proof.
The Court examines witness. Witness testifies.

Good cause appearing, the Court finds that the Plaintiff has proven its prima facie case and rules in favor of the Plaintiff against the Defendants jointly and severally as follows:

Damages $150,000.00
Pre-Judgment Interest at 10% $61,369.86
Costs $731.35
Attorney's Fees $0
Other $0

Total Judgment $212,101.21

Plaintiff to submit Proposed judgment on judicial council form for review and signature of the Court.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Default Judgment Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Aug-01-2025 Continued To Aug-20-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 505
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment.

Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Damon Carter; Dept. 505
Matter not reported.

Attorney Xin Huang appeared via zoom for the Plaintiff Yakov Pesochinsky.
Defendant Roman Orlov not appearing.

Mr. Huang states his witness (the Plaintiff) is not availabe to attend this hearing today. Mr. Huang requests a short continuance to give his client an oppertunity to participate.

This matter is continued to August 20, 2025 at 9:00am in dept. 505 for prove up hearing.

Counsel was reminded his client may appear via zoom for these hearings.
